title: localization — published macros
description: Blueprint macro paired with the `HasTranslations` trait for content-translation storage. Registered by LocalizationServiceProvider::boot() during the framework boot phase so every migration can call `$table->translations()` regardless of module priority.
namespace_note: Blueprint macros extend `Illuminate\Database\Schema\Blueprint`. The macro is registered on the framework blueprint class — no localization dependency is added to consumers because Blueprint is a Laravel core class.

macros.name: Blueprint::translations

columns.name: translations
columns.description: Content-translation JSONB blob. Shape: `{ locale: { field: value } }`. Read + written by the `HasTranslations` trait's accessors, which wrap `spatie/laravel-translatable` under the hood + layer on our attribute-driven metadata (#[Translatable]) + TenantLocale-aware fallback.

macros.description: Adds the single JSONB column that carries per-locale translations of content fields on any model composing `Stackra\Localization\Concerns\HasTranslations`. The macro is IDEMPOTENT and safe to call in either `create` or `table` migrations.
macros.boot_order_note: Registered by `LocalizationServiceProvider::boot()`. Because Laravel loads every ServiceProvider's `boot()` before running any migration, the macro is available to every migration regardless of the localization module's priority (66). Foundation-priority migrations (Wave 0-2) don't use this macro today, so the ordering is safe by construction.
macros.wire_shape: Standard: `data.name = 'Team'` (active-locale flat scalar). Admin editors request `?include=translations` to get the full `data.translations` blob for round-tripping.
macros.gin_index_note:
For tenants that query translations by locale (rare), consumers may add a GIN index on the JSONB column via a follow-up migration:
`$t->index([DB::raw("(translations->'$locale')")], 'plans_translations_locale_gin', 'gin');`
Default behaviour is no index — reads always target the active locale via in-process accessor, no query planner participation needed.

registration_order: Runs during the standard Laravel boot phase, BEFORE any migration is invoked. Priority ordering between modules only affects boot() invocation order, not macro availability to migrations — all providers finish booting before `php artisan migrate` runs the first CREATE TABLE.

migrated_from.reason: Trait + paired macro co-location convention. Foundation owns cross-cutting infrastructure traits (HasSystemFlag, HasUserstamps, HasAudit, etc) + their paired macros. `HasTranslations` is a domain-specific trait (content translation), so its paired macro belongs alongside the trait in localization.
